MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CHENG, ZHIYANG HEARING SET FOR NOV-21-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
DECLARATION OF NONY CONCEPCION IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLTF'S COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CONCEPCION, NONY V.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ZHIYANG CHENG'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND THE TRAFFIC COLLISION REPORT THAT IS ATTACHED AS AN EXHIBIT TO THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THIS SIMPLE CAR ACCIDENT CASE. THE TRAFFIC COLLISION REPORT SHOULD NOT BE ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND INCLUSION OF THE REPORT VIOLATES VEHICLE CODE 20013. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE REMAINING ITEMS BECAUSE THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1322(A) AND HIGHLIGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION PRECISELY WHAT ITEMS OUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: NONY CONCEPCION, 415.643.8933, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
DOMINIC POLITO, ESQ., 415.281.7108, FOR DEFENDANT ZHIYANG CHENG.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant ZHIYANG CHENG'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages and the traffic collision report that is attached as an exhibit to the Complaint. Plaintiff does not plead sufficient facts to support the prayer for punitive damages in this simple car accident case. The traffic collision report should not be attached to the complaint to allege a cause of action for negligence and inclusion of the report violates Vehicle Code 20013. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the remaining items because the moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1322(a) and highlight in the notice of motion precisely what items ought to be stricken. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
